agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Evolution of a delivery platform's core domain model from a 1:1 Shipment-to-Recipient relationship to a 1:N relationship. This is a fundamental structural change to the Shipment aggregate root, affecting data models, APIs, partner integrations, and downstream business processes like routing, proof-of-delivery, and billing.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management / Fulfillment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A unit of goods being transported from an origin to one or more destinations. The aggregate root that owns Recipients and their delivery preferences."},{"term":"Recipient","definition":"A person or entity designated to receive all or part of a Shipment. Identified by name, address, phone number, and delivery preferences."},{"term":"Delivery Preferences","definition":"Recipient-specific instructions governing how and when delivery should occur (e.g., time windows, safe-place authorization, signature requirements, contactless delivery)."},{"term":"Primary Recipient","definition":"The first (or sole) Recipient on a Shipment. For backward compatibility, the Recipient that legacy integrations and single-recipient APIs resolve to."},{"term":"Multi-Recipient Shipment","definition":"A Shipment with two or more Recipients, each potentially at different addresses with different delivery preferences."},{"term":"Split Delivery","definition":"The operational act of dividing a single Shipment's contents across multiple delivery stops for different Recipients."},{"term":"Shipment Status","definition":"The lifecycle state of a Shipment. In a multi-recipient model, status semantics become complex — the Shipment may be partially delivered."},{"term":"Proof of Delivery (POD)","definition":"Evidence that a delivery was completed. In a multi-recipient model, POD must be captured per-Recipient, not per-Shipment."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A Shipment MUST have at least one Recipient at all times.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A Shipment with zero Recipients has no delivery destination and is physically meaningless — the system state would be corrupt.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to remove the last Recipient from a Shipment must be rejected. Creating a Shipment with an empty Recipient list must fail validation."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Each Recipient on a Shipment MUST have a name, address, and phone number.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"These are the minimum fields required to execute a physical delivery. Without any one of them, the courier cannot complete the delivery.","testable_assertion":"Saving a Recipient with a null or empty name, address, or phone number must fail validation."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Each Recipient on a Shipment MUST have an independent set of delivery preferences.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"The entire purpose of the model change is per-Recipient preferences. Sharing preferences across Recipients defeats the business intent and creates incorrect delivery behavior.","testable_assertion":"Modifying one Recipient's delivery preferences must not affect any other Recipient on the same Shipment."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Shipment status must reflect the aggregate delivery state across all Recipients.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A Shipment reported as 'Delivered' when only 1 of 3 Recipients received their goods would be a data integrity violation and a customer trust breach.","testable_assertion":"A Shipment with N Recipients transitions to 'Delivered' only when all N Recipients have a completed delivery event. A Shipment is 'Partially Delivered' when at least one but not all Recipients have been delivered to."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Existing single-recipient Shipments must be migrated to the new model without data loss or behavioral change.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a migration policy, not a permanent system constraint. Once migration is complete, the rule is no longer relevant.","testable_assertion":"After migration, every pre-existing Shipment must have exactly one Recipient in the new recipients collection, with all original name/address/phone data preserved and delivery preferences set to system defaults."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"External partner APIs must continue to function for single-recipient Shipments without partner-side changes during a transition period.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a compatibility policy that will eventually expire when partners migrate to the new API contract.","testable_assertion":"Sending a Shipment with one Recipient through the legacy API format must produce a valid response indistinguishable from the pre-change API. The legacy endpoint must remain functional until all partners have migrated."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A multi-recipient Shipment exposed through a legacy single-recipient API must resolve to the Primary Recipient.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a backward-compatibility policy decision, not a physical or data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"When a multi-recipient Shipment is requested via the legacy API, the response must contain only the Primary Recipient's data. No error is thrown."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Proof of Delivery must be recorded per Recipient, not per Shipment.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"POD is legal and contractual evidence. Recording a single POD for multiple Recipients at different addresses would be factually incorrect and legally indefensible.","testable_assertion":"Each Recipient on a Shipment must have its own POD record. A Shipment-level POD query returns the collection of all per-Recipient PODs."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"No two Recipients on the same Shipment may have identical addresses unless explicitly allowed by the shipper.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Duplicate addresses are likely a user error but could be intentional (e.g., two departments at same building). This is a validation policy, not a hard constraint.","testable_assertion":"Adding a Recipient with an address matching an existing Recipient on the same Shipment triggers a warning or confirmation prompt, unless the shipper has opted out of duplicate-address checks."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A new Shipment is being created","when":"the creator specifies two or more Recipients with distinct addresses and delivery preferences","then":"the Shipment is created with all Recipients persisted, each with their own preferences, and the first Recipient is marked as Primary Recipient"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A Shipment exists with three Recipients","when":"delivery is completed for one Recipient","then":"the Shipment status transitions to 'Partially Delivered' and the individual Recipient's delivery status is 'Delivered'"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A Shipment exists with three Recipients and all have been delivered","when":"the last Recipient's delivery is confirmed","then":"the Shipment status transitions to 'Delivered'"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A Shipment exists with two Recipients","when":"a user attempts to remove both Recipients","then":"the system rejects the removal of the last Recipient and returns a validation error"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A pre-migration Shipment with one Recipient exists in production","when":"the data migration runs","then":"the Shipment has exactly one entry in the recipients collection with all original data intact and default delivery preferences applied"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A multi-recipient Shipment exists","when":"an external partner queries the Shipment via the legacy single-recipient API","then":"the response contains the Primary Recipient's data in the original response format with no schema changes"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A multi-recipient Shipment exists","when":"an external partner sends an update via the legacy API","then":"the update applies only to the Primary Recipient and other Recipients are unaffected"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A Shipment with two Recipients","when":"delivery to Recipient A fails and delivery to Recipient B succeeds","then":"POD is recorded only for Recipient B, Recipient A has a failed-delivery event, and Shipment status is 'Partially Delivered'"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A courier is assigned a multi-recipient Shipment","when":"they view the Shipment details on their device","then":"they see all Recipients with individual addresses, preferences, and stop sequence"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"A Recipient is removed from a Shipment after goods have already been dispatched toward their address","expected_behavior":"System must prevent removal of a Recipient once delivery is in-transit for that Recipient. The Recipient can only be cancelled, which triggers a return-to-sender or redirect flow."},{"scenario":"Two Recipients on the same Shipment have conflicting delivery time windows (e.g., morning-only vs evening-only) and are geographically close","expected_behavior":"Route optimization must treat them as separate stops with independent time constraints. The system should warn the shipper at creation time if fulfilling all preferences is infeasible within a single delivery run."},{"scenario":"A partner integration creates a Shipment via the legacy API, then a user adds a second Recipient via the new API","expected_behavior":"The Shipment now has two Recipients. Subsequent legacy API queries return only the Primary Recipient. The partner is unaware of the second Recipient, which is correct per BR-7."},{"scenario":"All Recipients on a Shipment have delivery failures","expected_behavior":"Shipment status becomes 'Failed' (not 'Partially Delivered'). Each Recipient has individual failure reasons recorded."},{"scenario":"A Shipment is created with 100 Recipients","expected_behavior":"The system should enforce a maximum Recipient count per Shipment (policy-level limit) to prevent abuse and operational infeasibility. The limit should be configurable."},{"scenario":"Billing and invoicing for a multi-recipient Shipment","expected_behavior":"Pricing model must account for multiple stops. A Shipment with 3 Recipients at 3 addresses is fundamentally a different cost structure than a single-recipient Shipment. Billing must be per-stop or per-Recipient, not flat per-Shipment."},{"scenario":"Webhook/event notifications to external systems that assume one delivery event per Shipment","expected_behavior":"Legacy webhook consumers receive a delivery event only when the entire Shipment is delivered (all Recipients). New webhook consumers can subscribe to per-Recipient delivery events."},{"scenario":"Data migration fails midway through the 2 million records","expected_behavior":"Migration must be idempotent and resumable. Partially migrated records must be identifiable. The system must support both old and new schema simultaneously during the migration window."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Significant. Multi-recipient Shipments open new revenue streams (consolidated multi-drop deliveries, B2B distribution). However, pricing models must be redesigned — flat per-Shipment pricing would undercharge multi-recipient Shipments. Migration and backward-compatibility engineering carry substantial development cost. Partner API changes may require renegotiation of integration contracts.","operational":"High complexity increase. Route optimization must handle multiple stops per Shipment. Courier workload estimation changes. Warehouse pick-and-pack processes may need splitting logic. Shipment status tracking becomes more complex with partial delivery states. Support teams need training on the new model.","user":"Shippers gain flexibility to send to multiple Recipients in one transaction. Couriers see more complex stop lists. Recipients may experience confusion if they receive partial Shipments without context. Admin/support dashboards must be redesigned to show per-Recipient status.","risk":"Data migration of 2M records is high-risk — any corruption breaks production. Partner API backward compatibility is critical — breaking partner integrations causes immediate revenue loss. The 'Partially Delivered' status is net-new and may confuse downstream reporting, SLA tracking, and customer communications. Domain model change touches the core aggregate, so nearly every service in the system is affected.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Shippers (B2B customers)","impact":"Primary beneficiaries. Can now send consolidated multi-recipient Shipments, reducing booking overhead and potentially cost."},{"group":"Recipients (end consumers)","impact":"May receive partial Shipments. Need clear communication about what they are receiving and when. Delivery preferences are now first-class, improving their experience."},{"group":"Couriers / Drivers","impact":"Multi-stop Shipments increase complexity. Route planning, proof-of-delivery, and failure handling all change. Training required."},{"group":"External API Partners","impact":"Must eventually migrate to new API contract. During transition, limited to single-recipient view of multi-recipient Shipments. Risk of data inconsistency if they assume 1:1 model."},{"group":"Finance / Billing Team","impact":"Pricing model must be redesigned. Revenue recognition per Shipment changes. Invoicing must reflect per-Recipient delivery outcomes."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Must handle new failure modes (partial delivery, per-Recipient issues). Need new tooling and training."},{"group":"Engineering / Platform Team","impact":"Core aggregate change affects nearly every bounded context. Migration, backward compatibility, and testing burden is substantial."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The product team's requirement is stated at a high level — 'one or more Recipients' with 'different delivery preferences' — but critical details are missing. What constitutes 'delivery preferences'? What is the maximum Recipient count? How should billing work? What is the partner migration timeline? The Shipment status model for partial delivery is not specified. These gaps create significant ambiguity that must be resolved before implementation.","high_confidence_areas":["The 1:N Recipient relationship is the correct structural change","At-least-one-Recipient invariant is fundamental","Per-Recipient POD is a hard requirement","Backward compatibility for partner APIs is necessary","Data migration of 2M records is required and high-risk"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact definition and schema of 'delivery preferences' — not specified by product team","Maximum number of Recipients per Shipment — no limit stated","Billing model for multi-recipient Shipments — not addressed","Partner API migration timeline and strategy — not defined","Whether 'Partially Delivered' is the correct status term or if a richer state model is needed","How Shipment-level SLAs apply when Recipients have different delivery outcomes"],"assumptions":["The first Recipient added is treated as Primary Recipient for backward compatibility","Delivery preferences include at minimum: time window, signature requirement, and safe-place authorization","The legacy API will remain available during a transition period rather than being immediately deprecated","Data migration will be online (no downtime) given 2M records in production"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Delivery preferences' is undefined. The product team says each Recipient 'can have different delivery preferences' but does not specify what preferences are available. This is the core of the feature and cannot be implemented without a defined schema. Options range from simple (time window + signature required) to complex (carrier preference, insurance level, package handling instructions, notification channels).","affected_stakeholders":["Shippers","Recipients","Couriers","Engineering"],"options":["Define a minimal preference set: delivery time window, signature requirement, safe-place authorization, contactless delivery flag","Define an extensible preference model with a base set plus custom key-value pairs","Defer to existing delivery-instructions fields and simply make them per-Recipient"],"recommendation":"Start with a well-defined minimal preference set that covers the top 5 most-requested delivery options. Make the schema extensible so new preferences can be added without model changes. Requires product team to provide the specific preference list."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Shipment status model for partial delivery is not specified. Current model likely has states like Created, Dispatched, In-Transit, Delivered, Failed. With multiple Recipients, a Shipment can be simultaneously delivered-to-A and failed-for-B. The product team has not defined how Shipment-level status should work in this scenario.","affected_stakeholders":["Shippers","Customer Support","Finance","Engineering"],"options":["Add a 'Partially Delivered' Shipment-level status and track per-Recipient status separately","Keep Shipment status as the worst-case (not delivered until all delivered) and add a per-Recipient status field","Replace Shipment-level status entirely with a computed aggregate of per-Recipient statuses"],"recommendation":"Introduce per-Recipient delivery status as the source of truth. Derive Shipment-level status as an aggregate: 'Delivered' only when all Recipients are delivered, 'Partially Delivered' when at least one is delivered, 'Failed' when all have failed. This preserves backward compatibility while adding precision."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Billing and pricing model for multi-recipient Shipments is not addressed. A Shipment to 3 addresses is operationally 3 deliveries. Charging the same as a single-recipient Shipment would be a revenue leak; charging 3x might not reflect shared pickup and trunk-haul costs.","affected_stakeholders":["Shippers","Finance","Sales"],"options":["Per-Recipient pricing: base fee + per-stop fee","Tiered pricing: discount per additional Recipient on the same Shipment","Keep current pricing and treat multi-recipient as a premium feature with a surcharge"],"recommendation":"Implement per-Recipient pricing with a shared base component (pickup + trunk haul) and per-stop last-mile fee. This reflects the actual cost structure and is transparent to shippers."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

content.thinking: Let me analyze this domain model evolution from single-recipient to multi-recipient shipments.

Requires product team to provide the specific preference list."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Shipment status model for partial delivery is not specified. Current model likely has states like Created, Dispatched, In-Transit, Delivered, Failed. With multiple Recipients, a Shipment can be simultaneously delivered-to-A and failed-for-B. The product team has not defined how Shipment-level status should work in this scenario.","affected_stakeholders":["Shippers","Customer Support","Finance","Engineering"],"options":["Add a 'Partially Delivered' Shipment-level status and track per-Recipient status separately","Keep Shipment status as the worst-case (not delivered until all delivered) and add a per-Recipient status field","Replace Shipment-level status entirely with a computed aggregate of per-Recipient statuses"],"recommendation":"Introduce per-Recipient delivery status as the source of truth. Derive Shipment-level status as an aggregate: 'Delivered' only when all Recipients are delivered, 'Partially Delivered' when at least one is delivered, 'Failed' when all have failed. This preserves backward compatibility while adding precision."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Billing and pricing model for multi-recipient Shipments is not addressed. A Shipment to 3 addresses is operationally 3 deliveries. Charging the same as a single-recipient Shipment would be a revenue leak; charging 3x might not reflect shared pickup and trunk-haul costs.","affected_stakeholders":["Shippers","Finance","Sales"],"options":["Per-Recipient pricing: base fee + per-stop fee","Tiered pricing: discount per additional Recipient on the same Shipment","Keep current pricing and treat multi-recipient as a premium feature with a surcharge"],"recommendation":"Implement per-Recipient pricing with a shared base component (pickup + trunk haul) and per-stop last-mile fee. This reflects the actual cost structure and is transparent to shippers."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

trace.result: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Evolution of a delivery platform's core domain model from a 1:1 Shipment-to-Recipient relationship to a 1:N relationship. This is a fundamental structural change to the Shipment aggregate root, affecting data models, APIs, partner integrations, and downstream business processes like routing, proof-of-delivery, and billing.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management / Fulfillment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A unit of goods being transported from an origin to one or more destinations. The aggregate root that owns Recipients and their delivery preferences."},{"term":"Recipient","definition":"A person or entity designated to receive all or part of a Shipment. Identified by name, address, phone number, and delivery preferences."},{"term":"Delivery Preferences","definition":"Recipient-specific instructions governing how and when delivery should occur (e.g., time windows, safe-place authorization, signature requirements, contactless delivery)."},{"term":"Primary Recipient","definition":"The first (or sole) Recipient on a Shipment. For backward compatibility, the Recipient that legacy integrations and single-recipient APIs resolve to."},{"term":"Multi-Recipient Shipment","definition":"A Shipment with two or more Recipients, each potentially at different addresses with different delivery preferences."},{"term":"Split Delivery","definition":"The operational act of dividing a single Shipment's contents across multiple delivery stops for different Recipients."},{"term":"Shipment Status","definition":"The lifecycle state of a Shipment. In a multi-recipient model, status semantics become complex — the Shipment may be partially delivered."},{"term":"Proof of Delivery (POD)","definition":"Evidence that a delivery was completed. In a multi-recipient model, POD must be captured per-Recipient, not per-Shipment."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A Shipment MUST have at least one Recipient at all times.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A Shipment with zero Recipients has no delivery destination and is physically meaningless — the system state would be corrupt.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to remove the last Recipient from a Shipment must be rejected. Creating a Shipment with an empty Recipient list must fail validation."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Each Recipient on a Shipment MUST have a name, address, and phone number.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"These are the minimum fields required to execute a physical delivery. Without any one of them, the courier cannot complete the delivery.","testable_assertion":"Saving a Recipient with a null or empty name, address, or phone number must fail validation."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Each Recipient on a Shipment MUST have an independent set of delivery preferences.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"The entire purpose of the model change is per-Recipient preferences. Sharing preferences across Recipients defeats the business intent and creates incorrect delivery behavior.","testable_assertion":"Modifying one Recipient's delivery preferences must not affect any other Recipient on the same Shipment."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Shipment status must reflect the aggregate delivery state across all Recipients.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A Shipment reported as 'Delivered' when only 1 of 3 Recipients received their goods would be a data integrity violation and a customer trust breach.","testable_assertion":"A Shipment with N Recipients transitions to 'Delivered' only when all N Recipients have a completed delivery event. A Shipment is 'Partially Delivered' when at least one but not all Recipients have been delivered to."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Existing single-recipient Shipments must be migrated to the new model without data loss or behavioral change.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a migration policy, not a permanent system constraint. Once migration is complete, the rule is no longer relevant.","testable_assertion":"After migration, every pre-existing Shipment must have exactly one Recipient in the new recipients collection, with all original name/address/phone data preserved and delivery preferences set to system defaults."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"External partner APIs must continue to function for single-recipient Shipments without partner-side changes during a transition period.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a compatibility policy that will eventually expire when partners migrate to the new API contract.","testable_assertion":"Sending a Shipment with one Recipient through the legacy API format must produce a valid response indistinguishable from the pre-change API. The legacy endpoint must remain functional until all partners have migrated."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A multi-recipient Shipment exposed through a legacy single-recipient API must resolve to the Primary Recipient.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a backward-compatibility policy decision, not a physical or data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"When a multi-recipient Shipment is requested via the legacy API, the response must contain only the Primary Recipient's data. No error is thrown."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Proof of Delivery must be recorded per Recipient, not per Shipment.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"POD is legal and contractual evidence. Recording a single POD for multiple Recipients at different addresses would be factually incorrect and legally indefensible.","testable_assertion":"Each Recipient on a Shipment must have its own POD record. A Shipment-level POD query returns the collection of all per-Recipient PODs."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"No two Recipients on the same Shipment may have identical addresses unless explicitly allowed by the shipper.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Duplicate addresses are likely a user error but could be intentional (e.g., two departments at same building). This is a validation policy, not a hard constraint.","testable_assertion":"Adding a Recipient with an address matching an existing Recipient on the same Shipment triggers a warning or confirmation prompt, unless the shipper has opted out of duplicate-address checks."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A new Shipment is being created","when":"the creator specifies two or more Recipients with distinct addresses and delivery preferences","then":"the Shipment is created with all Recipients persisted, each with their own preferences, and the first Recipient is marked as Primary Recipient"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A Shipment exists with three Recipients","when":"delivery is completed for one Recipient","then":"the Shipment status transitions to 'Partially Delivered' and the individual Recipient's delivery status is 'Delivered'"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A Shipment exists with three Recipients and all have been delivered","when":"the last Recipient's delivery is confirmed","then":"the Shipment status transitions to 'Delivered'"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A Shipment exists with two Recipients","when":"a user attempts to remove both Recipients","then":"the system rejects the removal of the last Recipient and returns a validation error"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A pre-migration Shipment with one Recipient exists in production","when":"the data migration runs","then":"the Shipment has exactly one entry in the recipients collection with all original data intact and default delivery preferences applied"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A multi-recipient Shipment exists","when":"an external partner queries the Shipment via the legacy single-recipient API","then":"the response contains the Primary Recipient's data in the original response format with no schema changes"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A multi-recipient Shipment exists","when":"an external partner sends an update via the legacy API","then":"the update applies only to the Primary Recipient and other Recipients are unaffected"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A Shipment with two Recipients","when":"delivery to Recipient A fails and delivery to Recipient B succeeds","then":"POD is recorded only for Recipient B, Recipient A has a failed-delivery event, and Shipment status is 'Partially Delivered'"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A courier is assigned a multi-recipient Shipment","when":"they view the Shipment details on their device","then":"they see all Recipients with individual addresses, preferences, and stop sequence"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"A Recipient is removed from a Shipment after goods have already been dispatched toward their address","expected_behavior":"System must prevent removal of a Recipient once delivery is in-transit for that Recipient. The Recipient can only be cancelled, which triggers a return-to-sender or redirect flow."},{"scenario":"Two Recipients on the same Shipment have conflicting delivery time windows (e.g., morning-only vs evening-only) and are geographically close","expected_behavior":"Route optimization must treat them as separate stops with independent time constraints. The system should warn the shipper at creation time if fulfilling all preferences is infeasible within a single delivery run."},{"scenario":"A partner integration creates a Shipment via the legacy API, then a user adds a second Recipient via the new API","expected_behavior":"The Shipment now has two Recipients. Subsequent legacy API queries return only the Primary Recipient. The partner is unaware of the second Recipient, which is correct per BR-7."},{"scenario":"All Recipients on a Shipment have delivery failures","expected_behavior":"Shipment status becomes 'Failed' (not 'Partially Delivered'). Each Recipient has individual failure reasons recorded."},{"scenario":"A Shipment is created with 100 Recipients","expected_behavior":"The system should enforce a maximum Recipient count per Shipment (policy-level limit) to prevent abuse and operational infeasibility. The limit should be configurable."},{"scenario":"Billing and invoicing for a multi-recipient Shipment","expected_behavior":"Pricing model must account for multiple stops. A Shipment with 3 Recipients at 3 addresses is fundamentally a different cost structure than a single-recipient Shipment. Billing must be per-stop or per-Recipient, not flat per-Shipment."},{"scenario":"Webhook/event notifications to external systems that assume one delivery event per Shipment","expected_behavior":"Legacy webhook consumers receive a delivery event only when the entire Shipment is delivered (all Recipients). New webhook consumers can subscribe to per-Recipient delivery events."},{"scenario":"Data migration fails midway through the 2 million records","expected_behavior":"Migration must be idempotent and resumable. Partially migrated records must be identifiable. The system must support both old and new schema simultaneously during the migration window."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Significant. Multi-recipient Shipments open new revenue streams (consolidated multi-drop deliveries, B2B distribution). However, pricing models must be redesigned — flat per-Shipment pricing would undercharge multi-recipient Shipments. Migration and backward-compatibility engineering carry substantial development cost. Partner API changes may require renegotiation of integration contracts.","operational":"High complexity increase. Route optimization must handle multiple stops per Shipment. Courier workload estimation changes. Warehouse pick-and-pack processes may need splitting logic. Shipment status tracking becomes more complex with partial delivery states. Support teams need training on the new model.","user":"Shippers gain flexibility to send to multiple Recipients in one transaction. Couriers see more complex stop lists. Recipients may experience confusion if they receive partial Shipments without context. Admin/support dashboards must be redesigned to show per-Recipient status.","risk":"Data migration of 2M records is high-risk — any corruption breaks production. Partner API backward compatibility is critical — breaking partner integrations causes immediate revenue loss. The 'Partially Delivered' status is net-new and may confuse downstream reporting, SLA tracking, and customer communications. Domain model change touches the core aggregate, so nearly every service in the system is affected.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Shippers (B2B customers)","impact":"Primary beneficiaries. Can now send consolidated multi-recipient Shipments, reducing booking overhead and potentially cost."},{"group":"Recipients (end consumers)","impact":"May receive partial Shipments. Need clear communication about what they are receiving and when. Delivery preferences are now first-class, improving their experience."},{"group":"Couriers / Drivers","impact":"Multi-stop Shipments increase complexity. Route planning, proof-of-delivery, and failure handling all change. Training required."},{"group":"External API Partners","impact":"Must eventually migrate to new API contract. During transition, limited to single-recipient view of multi-recipient Shipments. Risk of data inconsistency if they assume 1:1 model."},{"group":"Finance / Billing Team","impact":"Pricing model must be redesigned. Revenue recognition per Shipment changes. Invoicing must reflect per-Recipient delivery outcomes."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Must handle new failure modes (partial delivery, per-Recipient issues). Need new tooling and training."},{"group":"Engineering / Platform Team","impact":"Core aggregate change affects nearly every bounded context. Migration, backward compatibility, and testing burden is substantial."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The product team's requirement is stated at a high level — 'one or more Recipients' with 'different delivery preferences' — but critical details are missing. What constitutes 'delivery preferences'? What is the maximum Recipient count? How should billing work? What is the partner migration timeline? The Shipment status model for partial delivery is not specified. These gaps create significant ambiguity that must be resolved before implementation.","high_confidence_areas":["The 1:N Recipient relationship is the correct structural change","At-least-one-Recipient invariant is fundamental","Per-Recipient POD is a hard requirement","Backward compatibility for partner APIs is necessary","Data migration of 2M records is required and high-risk"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact definition and schema of 'delivery preferences' — not specified by product team","Maximum number of Recipients per Shipment — no limit stated","Billing model for multi-recipient Shipments — not addressed","Partner API migration timeline and strategy — not defined","Whether 'Partially Delivered' is the correct status term or if a richer state model is needed","How Shipment-level SLAs apply when Recipients have different delivery outcomes"],"assumptions":["The first Recipient added is treated as Primary Recipient for backward compatibility","Delivery preferences include at minimum: time window, signature requirement, and safe-place authorization","The legacy API will remain available during a transition period rather than being immediately deprecated","Data migration will be online (no downtime) given 2M records in production"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Delivery preferences' is undefined. The product team says each Recipient 'can have different delivery preferences' but does not specify what preferences are available. This is the core of the feature and cannot be implemented without a defined schema. Options range from simple (time window + signature required) to complex (carrier preference, insurance level, package handling instructions, notification channels).","affected_stakeholders":["Shippers","Recipients","Couriers","Engineering"],"options":["Define a minimal preference set: delivery time window, signature requirement, safe-place authorization, contactless delivery flag","Define an extensible preference model with a base set plus custom key-value pairs","Defer to existing delivery-instructions fields and simply make them per-Recipient"],"recommendation":"Start with a well-defined minimal preference set that covers the top 5 most-requested delivery options. Make the schema extensible so new preferences can be added without model changes. Requires product team to provide the specific preference list."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Shipment status model for partial delivery is not specified. Current model likely has states like Created, Dispatched, In-Transit, Delivered, Failed. With multiple Recipients, a Shipment can be simultaneously delivered-to-A and failed-for-B. The product team has not defined how Shipment-level status should work in this scenario.","affected_stakeholders":["Shippers","Customer Support","Finance","Engineering"],"options":["Add a 'Partially Delivered' Shipment-level status and track per-Recipient status separately","Keep Shipment status as the worst-case (not delivered until all delivered) and add a per-Recipient status field","Replace Shipment-level status entirely with a computed aggregate of per-Recipient statuses"],"recommendation":"Introduce per-Recipient delivery status as the source of truth. Derive Shipment-level status as an aggregate: 'Delivered' only when all Recipients are delivered, 'Partially Delivered' when at least one is delivered, 'Failed' when all have failed. This preserves backward compatibility while adding precision."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Billing and pricing model for multi-recipient Shipments is not addressed. A Shipment to 3 addresses is operationally 3 deliveries. Charging the same as a single-recipient Shipment would be a revenue leak; charging 3x might not reflect shared pickup and trunk-haul costs.","affected_stakeholders":["Shippers","Finance","Sales"],"options":["Per-Recipient pricing: base fee + per-stop fee","Tiered pricing: discount per additional Recipient on the same Shipment","Keep current pricing and treat multi-recipient as a premium feature with a surcharge"],"recommendation":"Implement per-Recipient pricing with a shared base component (pickup + trunk haul) and per-stop last-mile fee. This reflects the actual cost structure and is transparent to shippers."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
